evals.prompt: Set up a design system for a SaaS dashboard app. Create: design tokens (colors with primary/gray/semantic, spacing scale, typography), a Tailwind config that extends from the tokens, a Button component with variants (primary/secondary/ghost/danger) and sizes (sm/md/lg) using CVA, and a dark mode theme provider.
evals.expected_output: Token files, Tailwind config extending tokens, Button component with CVA variants and forwardRef, ThemeProvider with dark mode toggle, proper TypeScript types

assertions.name: token_files
assertions.description: Separate token definitions for colors, spacing, and typography as TypeScript constants

assertions.name: tailwind_extends_tokens
assertions.description: Tailwind config imports and extends from token files, not hardcoded values

assertions.name: cva_button
assertions.description: Button uses class-variance-authority (CVA) with variant and size props

assertions.name: forward_ref
assertions.description: Button uses forwardRef for DOM access

assertions.name: dark_mode
assertions.description: ThemeProvider or dark mode implementation with class toggle or CSS variables

assertions.name: accessibility
assertions.description: Button has focus-visible styles and aria attributes for loading/disabled states

evals.prompt: Build a form input system for our design system. Need: TextInput, Select, Checkbox, RadioGroup, and Textarea. Each must support label, help text, error state with message, disabled state, and required indicator. Use CVA for size variants (sm/md/lg), forwardRef, and Tailwind. Dark mode compatible.
evals.expected_output: Five form components with shared interface pattern, CVA variants, forwardRef, error/disabled/required states, dark mode classes, accessibility labels

assertions.name: all_five_components
assertions.description: TextInput, Select, Checkbox, RadioGroup, and Textarea components all created

assertions.name: cva_variants
assertions.description: At least TextInput and Select use CVA for size/variant props

assertions.name: forward_ref_on_inputs
assertions.description: Form inputs use forwardRef for DOM access and React Hook Form compatibility

assertions.name: error_state_with_message
assertions.description: Error prop renders an error message below the input with aria-invalid

assertions.name: disabled_state
assertions.description: Disabled prop grays out the input and prevents interaction

assertions.name: accessibility_labels
assertions.description: Labels use htmlFor, inputs have aria-describedby for help/error text

evals.prompt: Build a DataTable component for our design system. Needs: sortable columns (click header to sort asc/desc/none), row selection with header checkbox for select-all, column-level filtering, pagination (page selector + per-page dropdown), and loading skeleton state. Accept generic row data via TypeScript generics. Use Tailwind and CVA.
evals.expected_output: Generic DataTable<T> component with sortable headers, checkbox selection, filtering, pagination controls, skeleton loading state

assertions.name: typescript_generics
assertions.description: DataTable uses TypeScript generics for row data type (DataTable<T>)

assertions.name: sortable_columns
assertions.description: Column headers clickable to toggle asc/desc sort with visual indicator

assertions.name: row_selection
assertions.description: Row checkboxes and select-all header checkbox with onSelectionChange callback

assertions.name: pagination_controls
assertions.description: Page navigation and per-page limit selector with page count display

assertions.name: loading_skeleton
assertions.description: Skeleton rows shown during loading state

assertions.name: accessibility_roles
assertions.description: Table uses proper role attributes, sortable headers have aria-sort

evals.prompt: Build a toast notification system for our design system. Variants: success, error, warning, info. Features: auto-dismiss after configurable duration (default 5s), stack from bottom-right, manual dismiss with X button, optional action button (e.g., 'Undo'), max 5 visible toasts (oldest auto-dismissed). Use Zustand for toast state and a ToastProvider component.
evals.expected_output: Toast component with variants, Zustand toast store with add/dismiss, ToastProvider rendering portal, auto-dismiss timer, stacking, action button

assertions.name: four_variants
assertions.description: Success, error, warning, info variants with distinct colors/icons

assertions.name: zustand_store
assertions.description: Zustand store managing toast queue with addToast/dismissToast actions

assertions.name: auto_dismiss
assertions.description: Toasts auto-removed after configurable duration using setTimeout or timer

assertions.name: max_visible_limit
assertions.description: Maximum 5 toasts visible, oldest removed when limit exceeded

assertions.name: action_button_support
assertions.description: Toast accepts optional action prop with label and onClick handler

assertions.name: accessibility_live_region
assertions.description: Toast container uses role=status or aria-live for screen reader announcements

evals.prompt: Build a Modal/Dialog system for our design system. Sizes: sm (400px), md (600px), lg (800px), full (viewport). Features: focus trap inside modal, Escape key to close, backdrop click to close (configurable via closeOnBackdrop prop), body scroll lock when open, portal rendering to document.body. Use CVA for size variants, forwardRef on the content div.
evals.expected_output: Modal component with size variants, focus trap, Escape handling, configurable backdrop click, scroll lock, portal rendering

assertions.name: size_variants
assertions.description: CVA or conditional classes for sm/md/lg/full width variants

assertions.name: focus_trap
assertions.description: Tab key cycles focus within the modal, not escaping to background content

assertions.name: escape_to_close
assertions.description: Escape key triggers onClose callback

assertions.name: backdrop_click_configurable
assertions.description: closeOnBackdrop or closeOnOverlayClick prop controls whether backdrop click closes

assertions.name: body_scroll_lock
assertions.description: Document body overflow hidden when modal is open, restored on close

assertions.name: portal_rendering
assertions.description: Modal content rendered via React portal to document.body or a portal root
